assertions.reason: The browser surface still cannot prove the exact three-role JWT model or mutation-level requireRole() enforcement. Self-service signup only yielded researcher-level UI, even for reviewer/admin-themed accounts.
No browser-visible path produced an actual reviewer session. The reviewer-named self-service account still landed on the studies page with researcher controls such as Create Study.
No browser-visible path produced an actual admin session. The admin-named self-service account still hit the admin diagnostics access-denied page.
Researcher denial at /settings was still visible, but the admin-render half of the assertion remained untestable because no browser-visible admin claims path exists.
The real browser surface still exposes no visible way to obtain reviewer/admin JWT role claims; reviewer/admin-themed self-service signups continued to land in researcher-level UI.
The real browser surface still exposes no visible way to obtain reviewer/admin JWT role claims; reviewer/admin-themed self-service signups continued to land in researcher-level UI.

blockers.description: Self-service signup still never surfaced reviewer/admin claims in the real browser UI.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Created new researcher/reviewer/admin-themed accounts through the browser; reviewer/admin-themed accounts still rendered researcher-level UI and admin diagnostics access denied.

summary: Researcher-side restrictions still render correctly, but reviewer/admin role validation remains blocked because self-service browser flows never expose non-researcher claims.
